diagrams.description: This timeline traces the major milestones and transitions of Ancient History from the invention of writing to the fall of the Western Roman Empire. It begins with the emergence of Sumerian city‑states (c. 3500‑3000 BCE) and the unification of Egypt (c. 3100 BCE). The Bronze Age (c. 3000‑1200 BCE) saw Hammurabi’s Code (c. 1754), the Minoans and Mycenaeans, and the Shang Dynasty in China. The Iron Age and Axial Age (c. 1200‑500 BCE) brought the rise of Assyria, the Phoenician alphabet, and foundational thinkers like Confucius and Buddha. Classical Antiquity (c. 500‑323 BCE) encompassed Greek democracy, the Persian Wars, and Alexander’s conquest. The Hellenistic and Roman Republican periods (323‑27 BCE) witnessed the spread of Greek culture and Rome’s expansion. The Roman Empire and Late Antiquity (27 BCE‑476 CE) saw the Pax Romana, the spread of Christianity, and the final division and fall of the West.
